# Project Context

> This file is the single source of truth for AI-assisted sessions on this project.
> Paste this into a new chat before starting. Update it at the end of every session using /session-close.
> Keep it concise — if it grows beyond ~60 lines, summarize older sections.

---

## What this project is

<!-- One paragraph. What is the goal, the scientific question, and the approach. -->

## Current state

<!-- What works, what is stubbed, what is broken, what is untested. Be honest. -->

## Key decisions made (and why)

<!-- List the important architectural, algorithmic, or design choices.
     For each one: what was decided, what was the alternative, and why was this chosen.
     Future-you needs the WHY, not just the WHAT. -->

## Invariants that must not be violated

<!-- Mathematical, physical, or architectural constraints that are non-negotiable.
     E.g. "The wavefunction must remain normalized.", "No data leakage across train/val split." -->

## Known bad approaches (and why)

<!-- What looks tempting but does not work, based on experiments or reasoning.
     This is the most valuable section for AI context — it prevents re-suggesting paths already ruled out. -->

## Open questions

<!-- What is unresolved? What do you not understand well enough yet? -->

## Next steps

<!-- What should happen in the next session, in priority order. -->

## References and key files

<!-- Papers, documentation, or files that are essential context.
     Use relative paths for files: e.g. src/models/wavefunction.py -->
